tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, glucosuria, bilirubinuria, mild ketonuria, and low specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal concentrating ability and possible tubular dysfunction. The urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, but no evidence of infection. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or anemia. Overall, findings are suggestive of renal dysfunction with abnormal urinary findings; further nephrological evaluation is recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
